DU.pitch: Andrew — you said it'd be awesome to see a running deployment of what we've built. Here it is. Killinchu is counter-UAS sense-and-evidence that ships as a UDS Zarf package, deploys into UDS Core airgapped, and emits cosign-attested Khipu receipts on the exact supply-chain surface your team builds — Zarf, Pepr, NeuVector. We detect, classify, and geolocate drones from the signals they emit, sign every decision with a real ECDSA-P256 DSSE receipt, and hand a court-admissible Body-of-Evidence to the authorized customer. We don't jam, spoof, or hijack — that's the customer's lawful authority. We're the verifiable governance overlay on top of your air-gap future.
DU.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.

CDAO.pitch: Killinchu gives your Agent Network the one thing the primes don't ship: interpretable, auditable agent decisions. Every sense-and-evidence decision emits a cosign-verifiable receipt on a Lean-proved total-order chain — cutting or reordering is mathematically observable. It's a Responsible-AI governance overlay for agentic battle-management, deployable airgapped via UDS, with a 13-axis trust gate where a legality-zero event is an unconditional hard reject.
CDAO.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.

DIU.pitch: For the CUAS networked kill-chain, Killinchu stays strictly in the post-quantum encryption, governance, and receipt-chain layer. We provide packet-level data protection, a TAK-compatible signed receipt chain, and audit-log verification for the prime's network. No kinetic targeting, no fire-control, no AiTR — effector selection stays with the prime. Lambda-axis governance attaches as a verification and audit overlay only. That's the lawful commercial sweet spot.
DIU.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.

AFWERX.pitch: Killinchu is edge-deployable counter-UAS governance: it runs fully airgapped via a UDS Zarf bundle, puts Lambda-axis policy gates on field-updatable edge models, and emits signed evidence receipts at the tactical edge. SBIR Phase I to STRATFI/TACFI on-ramp — the governance overlay that makes any edge-AI autonomy defensible, with cosign attestation and a 749/14/163 doctrine baseline.
AFWERX.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.

Anduril.pitch: Killinchu is a sensor-fusion and governance overlay that complements Lattice — not a C2 competitor. We ingest Remote-ID, ADS-B, MAVLink, and RF passively, fuse them onto a Cesium globe, and attach a cosign-verifiable receipt to every track and decision. Where Lattice mesh moves the data, Killinchu proves the governance: signed Body-of-Evidence, a 13-axis gate, and after-action replay. We sense and evidence; the authorized operator acts.
Anduril.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.

Scale.pitch: Scale primes Thunderforge, but no prime ships a publicly-auditable, proof-carrying governance surface. Killinchu is that overlay: signed Khipu receipts, a 13-axis Lambda gate, and a Lean-proved total-order chain that make agentic defense decisions interpretable and auditable under DoW Responsible-AI policy. UDS-deployable, cosign-attested, airgap-ready.
Scale.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.

ShieldAI.pitch: Hivemind flies; Killinchu senses and evidences. As a partnership overlay for EdgeOS Pilot blocks, Killinchu attaches Lambda-gated decision receipts and a signed Body-of-Evidence for after-action review on V-BAT and MQ-20 missions. Two-person Yuyay gate for any own-fleet action, cosign-attested, deployable airgapped via UDS. Governance and explainability, not a competing autonomy stack.
ShieldAI.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.

Replicator.pitch: Killinchu rides on the substrate you already operate: it deploys via Zarf into OpenShift and UDS Core, takes its signing keys from Vault, and adds the Lambda-gated, cosign-attested receipt chain that none of the integrator pipelines ship. For resilient C2 and autonomous teaming, it's the verifiable governance layer — complementary to aiSSEMBLE, OpenShift AI, and Lattice, not a replacement.
Replicator.disclaimer: WE SENSE. WE EVIDENCE. WE DO NOT JACK INTO THIRD-PARTY DRONES. See /api/killinchu/v2/legal.
